replies.author: @Devilsblight86
replies.text:
​@YTChannel420You missing out on some good anime. Not all of them are the weird shit you keep hearing about. Let me give you 3 or 4 anime that I hope wil change your mind: 

Monster: A thriller anime about a surgeon who saves a young child from an accident only to find out that same boy grows up to be a serial killer and he has to stop him.

 FullMetal Alchemist (both the original and the Brotherhood remake): A tale of two brothers in a world where alchemy is the dominant science try to use it to resurrect their dead mother. Things go wrong. They also uncover a plot that could their their world apart.

Demon Slayer: A young Japanese mountain boy finds his family murdered by a demon and worse of all, his surviving younger is turned into a demon herself. Now he has to join an organization that slays demons for a way to save his sister and avenge his family. (This one I'm currently enjoying and there's a movie that's coming to the US that starts off the final arc)

Death Note: A really popular one. This one is about a kid who finds a notebook that allows him to kill a person just by writing their name into it. He decides to use it in order to become the God of a New World, only for his actions to get the attention of the police and a famous detective by the name of L.

Indeed. This crap started in the 90s with a southern pastor who was so offended by Stern, he pressured radio stations and the fcc to stop Stern's show. He didn't turn the knob and stop listening, he hate-listened the radio and hate-warltched on E! and began the cancelling. It was what primarily prompted Sterns move to Sirius in the first place.

Then people went hey, we can do this to this or that person they don't like. I do not understand why people don't avoid what they dislike or disagree with and spend their time with what they do like.  No life I imagine, so this makes them feel important.

author: @JCWinters-bu8de
text:
The US Censorship Bills to go against so far are:

S.1748 - Kids Online Safety Act
S.1409 - Kids Online Safety Act
S.737 - SCREEN Act
H.R.1623 - SCREEN Act
S.5364 - App Store Accountability Act
H.R.791 - Foreign Anti-Digital Piracy Act
Block BEARD Act


The Bill to approve so far is,

S.401 - Fair Access to Banking Act

author: @ed-bar
text: This is awesome, but I have to be honest. If Steam would have shown a little backbone, none of this would be an issue. They've been going after my book distributor for decades, every few years, they would give them a list of topics they weren't allowed to publish, or they would shut down their payment operations. Every time they did this, my distributor would tell them to fuck off, and fuck off they did. Nothing ever got censored, payments always went through. And all Mark did was occassionally tell them to fuck off again. Steam rolled over, Steam chose to censor. End of story.

author: @Rigdereine
text: The EO instructs regulators of banks, but Visa/Mastercard are private networks with their own global rules and contracts. author: @Cortanis001
text: The one issue I have with this is that it was done through an EO. This should have gone through the courts and legally been ruled on so that the next person in office can't pull a 180 on this as a policy. There are plenty of laws from monopoly laws to the easy tortious interference charge for pulling these stunts. Further more, they should have pushed for the online space to be recognized as protected under the First Amendment by the courts. Until legal precedent is set or it's put into law by legislation, it's not a secure ruling.

author: @carried9130
text:
This crap started in the 90s with a pastor who was so offended by Stern, he pressured radio stations and the fcc to stop Stern's show. He didn't turn the knob and stop listening, he hate-listened the radio and hate-watched on E! and began the cancelling we see today. It was what primarily prompted Sterns move to Sirius in the first place. He laughed it off at first but as they pressured and interfered, he said eff that. 

I didn't have Sirius, so I only got clips and reruns on YT after he left radio...but his complete personality switch/ mask drop made him repellant. I just don't watch or listen. Those who like him, enjoy.

Then people went hey, we can do this to this or that person they don't like. I do not understand why people don't avoid what they dislike or disagree with and spend their time with what they do like.
